与使用我们的Gretel测试版和开源合成数据库的开发人员合作时，我们听到的最重要的要求之一是，开发人员要求我们极其简单地开始使用包括数据匿名化、隐私工程和数据平衡在内的用例。本周，我们非常高兴地发布了Gretel的蓝图。Gretel蓝图是示例代码和示例数据集的集合，这些示例代码和示例数据集利用Gretel的SDK，可以轻松地调整以解决客户特定的用例。
来自我们社区的反馈使我们相信代码的应用实际上可以是特定于用例的。但开发人员不应该总是为了解决特定问题而做出一系列技术决策。
就像客户用例会发展一样，蓝图也会发展。我们将根据新功能和客户反馈添加新的蓝图-如果您想要查看您的用例蓝图，请在Twitter上通知我们。我们的几个蓝图也将有自己的详细博客，深入探讨用例，更重要的是，您如何使用Gretel自己解决这些挑战。
Gretel蓝图将通过Gretel Cloud访问，并托管在GitHub上。很快，在创建Gretel云项目时，您就可以选择我们的蓝图作为起始模板，几张蓝图将自带样本数据。如果您已经有一个项目或正在使用您自己的数据创建一个空白项目，则可以从“转换”页面获得蓝图。
进入转换页面后，您将能够选择任何蓝图与您当前的Gretel云项目一起使用或与您自己环境中的数据一起使用。
与我们的核心开源SDK一样，蓝图也托管在GitHub上，并在Apache2.0下获得许可。我们这样做是为了让客户可以采用我们的样例代码来具体满足他们的需求，而不会过度规定如何解决任何一个问题。
在接下来的几天里，我们将发布我们新的Gretel云工作流程，并为我们的许多蓝图发布教程、演练，甚至是客户推荐信。如果你喜欢，给我们一个关于吉瑟布的⭐！请继续关注我们的博客和Twitter，了解这些令人兴奋的公告，并随时通过[受保护的电子邮件]、GitHub或社交媒体联系我们的团队！
note_en:
Working with developers using our  Gretel beta and open-source  synthetic data libraries, one of the top requests we have heard is developers asking us to make it incredibly simple to get started on use cases including  data anonymization,  privacy engineering, and  data balancing. This week we are thrilled to release Gretel Blueprints. Gretel Blueprints are collections of sample code and sample datasets that utilize Gretel's SDKs that can be easily adapted to solve customer-specific use cases.
 Feedback from our community has led us to believe that the application of code can actually be use case specific.  Developers should not have to always make a series of technology decisions in order to solve a specific problem.
 Just like customer use cases evolve, so will Blueprints. We will be adding new Blueprints based on new features and customer feedback- let us know on  Twitter if you would like to see Blueprints for your use case.  Several of our Blueprints will also have detailed blogs of their own to deep dive into the use case and more importantly,  how you can use Gretel to solve these challenges on your own.
  Gretel Blueprints will be accessible via  Gretel Cloud and are hosted on  GitHub.  Soon, when creating a Gretel Cloud Project, you will be able to choose our Blueprints as a starting template and several Blueprints will come with their own sample data. If you have a project already or are creating a blank project with your own data, the Blueprints will be available from the "Transform" page.
 Once in the "Transform" page, you will be able to select any Blueprint to use with your current Gretel Cloud Project or for use with data in your own environment.
   Like our core open source SDKs, Blueprints are hosted on  GitHub and licensed under Apache 2.0. We did this so customers can adopt our sample code to meet their needs specifically without being overly prescriptive on how to solve any one problem.
 In the coming days, we'll be making announcements on our new Gretel Cloud workflows and releasing tutorials, walk-throughs, and even customer testimonials for many of our Blueprints. If you like it, give us a ⭐ on  Github! Stay tuned to our  Blog and  Twitter for these exciting announcements and feel free to reach out via   [email protected], GitHub, or social media to engage with our team!
title: 蓝图，只需点击几下即可轻松匿名和平衡数据集
title_en: Blueprints, making it easy to anonymize and balance datasets with a few clicks

GAME Basic for Sega saturn是一个自制的开发工具包，允许你使用Basic编程语言为世嘉土星编写游戏。如果你熟悉PlayStation的Net Yaroze平台，可以将其视为土星的答案-只是更便宜，更容易上手。
Game Basic使用BASIC语言在编程技能方面的入门门槛非常低。虽然土星的编程难度是出了名的高，但Game Basic让它很容易上手，而且功能强大得令人惊讶，允许非常轻松的精灵操作和简单的3D多边形实现。它甚至包括一根适配器电缆，允许你从PC与土星进行通信，以传输或保存程序并简化开发。例如，下面是一个Pilotwings风格的演示，但使用的是Game Basic：
注意：Game Basic仅在日本发布，因此这意味着可能很难获得完整的设置，并且所有文档都是日语的！此外，允许您使用PC进行简化开发的支持软件是为Windows 95时代设计的，并且完全不会安装在现代系统上。哦，让您将土星连接到PC的适配器电缆是25针串行连接！
世界上还有谁同时拥有Game Basic和带物理串口的Windows95 PC？没有人！(好吧，除非你是现代复古玩家)但如果你是一个勇敢的实验者，不怕稍微修修补补，那么即使是今天，仍然有多种选择可以让一切正常工作！你甚至可以通过仿真做很多事情。所以，让我们去实验室开始…吧。
使用Game Basic有几种选择，既有非常简单但笨重的，也有非常强大和精简的。以下是三个选项：
能够玩日本土星游戏(日式或改装主机、Pro Action Replay/伪土星凯盒或土星模拟器)。
土星内部存储器中有足够的空间(如果你想保存你的程序)。
对于此选项，您将在没有PC连接的土星上运行Game Basic，只使用标准的土星附件。如果您只想编写“Hello，World！”风格的程序或使用工具包附带的整齐的游戏和演示，这是一个合理的选择。理论上，您甚至可以用这种方式编写最复杂的程序，但您会遇到保存到土星内部存储器的游戏大小的限制。此外，使用虚拟键盘编程是非常痛苦的。如果您没有其他选项所需的硬件，请从这里开始。或者如果你只是想随便看看，看看这是怎么回事。
Game Basic的一个伟大之处在于，访问土星的外围设备非常容易，包括内部备份RAM、外部存储盒，甚至土星软盘驱动器。有了这样的设置，你将有足够的空间来保存程序，还可以使用真正的键盘输入文本。你甚至可以开始尝试不同形式的输入，比如模拟控制和光枪！但如果没有PC提供的工具，就很难制作出好看的精灵、纹理和3D模型。因此，你甚至可以开始尝试不同形式的输入，比如模拟控制和光枪！但如果不能访问PC提供的工具，就很难制作出好看的精灵、纹理和3D模型。因此，你甚至可以开始尝试不同形式的输入，比如模拟控制和光枪！但如果没有PC提供的工具，就很难制作出好看的精灵、纹理和3D模型。尽管如此，这仍然会限制你的能力。无论如何，对于纯粹的乐趣因素“嘿，看！”来说，这是一个很好的选择。我在用我的土星编程！“。或者如果你没有能力将土星连接到个人电脑上。
你甚至可以用土星模拟器走这条路，让你很容易获得改进的键盘、鼠标和存储选项。“我已经确认Mednafen成功地模拟了Game Basic，并允许键盘和鼠标直通输入，这意味着你可以进行大量的土星开发，进入门槛很小。
一台带USB端口的现代PC，能够运行虚拟机(我使用的是VirtualBox)。
能够在原装硬件上玩日本土星游戏(日式或改装游戏机，或Pro Action Replay/伪土星凯盒)。
也可以选择任何有趣的土星配件(我特别推荐使用键盘或键盘适配器)。
这就是凯迪拉克选项！这就是我使用的设置，这就是游戏Basic的实际用途(我想除了没人想到它会在VM上运行之外)，也是本指南其余部分将重点关注的。使用这种设置，编写游戏就像在文本编辑器中编写Basic并点击几个按钮将其发送到您的土星一样简单，在那里它会立即显示在您的电视上，并对控制器和键盘输入做出响应！说真的，一旦您让它运行…，它就会变得超级酷。
简单而增强的土星专用设置非常简单。“你只需像任何其他土星游戏一样启动Game Basic并开始，所以没有太多的配置要讨论。”不管你选择什么设置，都可以继续到第2部分进行几个测试程序。但是如果你想要完整的设置，这是一个相当大的项目要开始进行，所以请继续阅读第3部分，了解完整的操作方法！(但是如果你想要完整的设置，这是一个相当大的项目，所以请继续阅读第三部分，了解完整的操作方法！)
note_en:
Game BASIC for Sega Saturn is a homebrew development kit that allows you to program games for the Sega Saturn using the BASIC programming language.  If you’re familiar with the PlayStation’s Net Yaroze platform, think of this as the Saturn’s answer to it – just cheaper and easier to get started with.
 Game BASIC’s use of the BASIC language makes for a very low barrier to entry in terms of programming skill.  Though the Saturn is notoriously difficult to program for, Game BASIC makes it easy to get started and is surprisingly powerful, allowing very easy sprite manipulation and straightforward 3D polygon implementation.  It even includes an adapter cable that allows you to communicate with the Saturn from your PC to transfer or save programs and streamline development. For example, here’s a Pilotwings-esque demo, but in Game BASIC:
  The caveat?  Game BASIC was released only in Japan, so this means a complete setup can be difficult to obtain and all documentation is in Japanese!  Moreover, the supporting software that allows you to use your PC for streamlined development was intended for the Windows 95 era and flat out does not install on modern systems. Oh, and the adapter cable that allows you to connect your Saturn to your PC is a 25-pin serial connection!
 Who in the world still has both Game BASIC and a Windows 95 PC with a physical serial port? Nobody!  (Well, unless you’re  Modern Vintage Gamer) But if you’re a brave experimenter who’s not afraid to tinker a bit, there are still multiple options to get everything working, even today!  You can even do a lot just via emulation.  So, let’s head to the Lab and get started…
   There are several options for working with Game BASIC, ranging from quite simple but clunky to work with, to quite powerful and streamlined.  Here are the three options:
   The ability to play Japanese Saturn games (A Japanese or modded console, a Pro Action Replay/ Pseudo Saturn Kai cartridge, or a Saturn emulator)
  Plenty of room in the Saturn’s internal memory (if you want to save your programs)
 For this option, you’ll run Game BASIC on the Saturn with no PC connection, using only standard Saturn accessories.  This is a reasonable choice if you just want to write “Hello, World!”-style programs or play around with the neat games and demos that come with the kit.  Theoretically, you can write even the most complex programs this way, but you’ll run into limitations on the size of games you can save to the Saturn’s internal memory.  Plus, programming with a virtual keyboard is an absolute pain.  Start here if you don’t have the necessary hardware for the other options, or if you just want to poke around a bit and see what this is all about.
    One of the great things about Game BASIC is how easy it makes it to access the Saturn’s peripherals, including the internal backup RAM, external memory cartridges, and even the Saturn Floppy Disk Drive.  With a setup like this, you’ll have plenty of space to save your programs and you can use a real keyboard for text entry.  You can even start experimenting with different forms of input, like analog controls and light guns!  But without access to the tools a PC provides, it will be difficult to make nice-looking sprites, textures, and 3D models.  So, this will still limit what you’re capable of.  Regardless, this is a great option for the sheer fun factor of “Hey look! I’m programming with my Saturn!” or if you have no ability to connect your Saturn to a PC.
 You can even go this route with a Saturn emulator, giving you easy access to improved keyboard, mouse, and storage options.  I’ve confirmed that Mednafen successfully emulates Game BASIC and allows for keyboard and mouse pass-through input, meaning you can do a whole lot of Saturn development with very little barrier to entry.
   A modern PC with a USB port, capable of running a Virtual Machine (I use VirtualBox)
   The ability to play Japanese Saturn games on original hardware (Japanese or modded console, or a Pro Action Replay/Pseudo Saturn Kai cartridge)
  Optionally, any fun Saturn accessories you may want to experiment with (I especially recommend a keyboard or keyboard adapter)
 This is the Cadillac option!  This is the setup I use, is how Game BASIC was really intended to be used (well, except nobody expected it to be run on a VM, I suppose), and is what the rest of this guide will focus on.  With this setup, writing a game is as simple as writing BASIC in a text editor and hitting a couple of buttons to send it to your Saturn, where it immediately shows up on your TV and responds to controller and keyboard input!  Seriously, it’s super cool once you get it working…
 The Simple and Enhanced Saturn-only setups are extremely straightforward.  You just boot Game BASIC like any other Saturn game and get started, so there’s not much configuration to discuss.  Regardless of the setup you choose, continue on to Part 2 for a few test programs.  But if you want the Full setup, it’s quite a project to get going, so read on to Part 3 for the complete How-To!
title: 世嘉土星自酿游戏基础版
title_en: Sega Saturn Homebrew with Game Basic

根据本周将听取行业高管意见的一个关键委员会成员的一份新报告，一些参议院民主党人正在寻求让监管机构能够保护当地新闻媒体，指责谷歌(Google)和Facebook等科技巨头“不公平的商业行为”。
这是在参议院商务委员会周三与Alphabet公司的谷歌、Facebook公司首席执行长举行听证会前夕发布的。Twitter Inc.的报告认为，当地新闻业正处于危机之中-冠状病毒大流行严重加剧了危机-部分原因是所谓的科技平台的反竞争行为，这些行为应该受到联邦贸易委员会(Federal Trade Commission)的监管。
“他们正面临着不公平的做法，”该委员会的资深成员、华盛顿州民主党参议员玛丽亚·坎特维尔(Maria Cantwell)在接受采访时说。“我们认为国会和联邦贸易委员会应该解决这些不公平的做法。”
谷歌的一位女发言人说，这份报告“歪曲了围绕谷歌在在线新闻生态系统中的角色以及我们为面临向数字世界转型挑战的出版商提供的价值的现实”。
Facebook驳斥了该报告的指控，即科技巨头为自己的网站窃取当地新闻内容和数据，这种做法被称为抓取。Facebook的一位发言人说：“关于我们抄袭新闻文章的指责根本不是真的。”
note_en:
Some Senate Democrats are seeking to enable regulators to protect local news outlets, accusing tech giants such as Google and Facebook of “unfair business practices,” according to a new report by members of a key committee that will hear from the top industry executives this week.
  Released on the  eve of the Senate Commerce Committee’s hearing Wednesday with the chief executives of Alphabet Inc.’s Google, Facebook Inc. and Twitter Inc., the report argues that local journalism is in a crisis— badly exacerbated by the coronavirus pandemic—partly because of alleged anticompetitive behavior on the part of the tech platforms that ought to be policed by the Federal Trade Commission.
  “They are facing unfair practices,” Sen. Maria Cantwell (D., Wash.), the ranking member on the committee, said in an interview. “We think Congress and the FTC should address those unfair practices.”
  A Google spokeswoman said the report “distorts the reality around Google’s role in the online news ecosystem and the value that we provide to publishers who face challenges in transitioning to the digital world.”
  Facebook took issue with the report’s allegation that tech giants take local news content and data for their own sites, a practice known as scraping. “The accusation that we scrape news articles is simply not true,” a Facebook spokesman said.
title: 参议院报告称谷歌的AMP应由联邦贸易委员会监管
title_en: Senate report says Google's AMP should be policed by the FTC

我们很兴奋地宣布发布1.20版。1.20版本于2020年10月26日发布。此版本提供了新功能，并为1.19版及更早版本的用户解决了问题。
除了错误修复和用户界面改进之外，还有几个值得关注的新功能和改进。
当FusionAuth充当SP或IdP时，它现在支持SAML POST绑定。这使得FusionAuth可以与许多流行的商业现成应用程序和网站集成，包括login.gov和Alfresh co。
FusionAuth还支持SessionIndex属性，该属性是可选的，但某些软件应用程序(特别是ArtiFactory)需要该属性。
最后，FusionAuth现在支持SAML请求中的AssertionConsumer erServiceURL。还可以在应用程序SAML设置中配置多个重定向URL。
在这个版本中，我们将docker的基本镜像从alpine更新为ubuntu：ocus。这应该不会影响功能，但如果您正在基于我们的镜像构建Docker镜像，则值得注意。
我们进行此更改是因为为了在不包含GNU C库(Glibc)的情况下在Alpine上运行，我们必须使用使用MUSL C库编译的OpenJDK的自定义版本。由于此选项可能存在一些性能问题，我们已转向由AdoptOpenJDK提供的正式版本的JDK。此构建是使用glibc编译的。不幸的是，使用ubuntu：ocus图像时，docker图像的大小比基于alpine的图像增加了大约30MB。然而，在我们从AdoptOpenJDK获得基于MUSL C库的正式构建之前，出于性能方面的考虑，我们不打算在Alpine上发布官方FusionAuth映像。
在此版本中，我们添加了一些新表单。这些定制也仅对付费版的客户可用。(了解有关购买付费版的更多信息。)
这个新特性允许您自定义用于从FusionAuth管理UI添加或编辑用户的表单。这些表单可以按租户配置。例如，当FusionAuth管理员创建用户时，您现在可以需要手机号码，或者需要一个或多个自定义字段。
您还可以在FusionAuth管理UI中修改注册创建和更新表单，包括捕获自定义数据、删除未使用的字段以及向自定义字段添加验证。此表单可根据应用程序逐个配置。
例如，如果您的业务逻辑需要知道用户最喜欢的颜色(或任何其他颜色)，FusionAuth可以帮助您：
当与FusionAuth的细粒度角色(允许您创建具有有限权限的用户)结合使用时，您可以将这些表单公开给客户服务代表或您可能不想访问整个FusionAuth管理UI的其他用户。
除了这些功能之外，还有十多个其他错误也得到了解决，GitHub的问题也得到了解决。这些修复包括在某些情况下对JWT内容的更改、仪表板用户数和多余的日志消息。有关1.19和1.20之间的更改的完整细目，请参阅发行说明。
如果您想升级您的自托管FusionAuth实例，请参阅我们的升级指南。如果您部署了FusionAuth云，请从您的帐户控制面板打开支持请求或使用我们的联系人表单，我们将升级您的服务器！或者，如果您想下载并使用FusionAuth，请查看您的选项。
note_en:
We’re excited to announce the release of version 1.20. The 1.20 release shipped on Oct 26, 2020. This version delivers new features as well as resolving issues for users on version 1.19 and older.
  In addition to bug fixes and user interface improvements, there are a couple of new features and improvements which deserve a spotlight.
   FusionAuth now has support for SAML POST bindings when it is acting as either the SP or the IdP. This allows FusionAuth to integrate with a number of popular commercial off-the-shelf applications and websites, including  login.gov and  Alfresco.
 FusionAuth also supports the  SessionIndex attribute, which is optional, but required by some software applications, notably Artifactory.
 Finally FusionAuth now honors the  AssertionConsumerServiceURL in a SAML request. Multiple redirect URLs can be configured in the application SAML settings as well.
  In this release, we updated the base image for Docker from  alpine to  ubuntu:focal. This should not impact functionality, but it’s worth paying attention to if you are building Docker images based on our image.
 We made this change because in order to run on  alpine without including the GNU C Library ( glibc) we had to use a custom build of OpenJDK compiled using the  musl C library. Due to some possible performance concerns with this option, we have moved to an official build of JDK provided by AdoptOpenJDK. This build is compiled using  glibc. Unfortunately, using the  ubuntu:focal image increased the docker image size by approximately 30 MB over the  alpine based image. However, until we can obtain official builds from AdoptOpenJDK based upon the  musl C library, we are not planning to ship an official FusionAuth image on  alpine due to the performance concerns.
   In this release, we’ve added a few new forms. These customizations are also only available to customers with a paid edition. (Learn more about  purchasing a paid edition.)
 This new feature allows you to customize the form used to add or edit a user from the FusionAuth admin UI. These forms can be configured on a per tenant basis. For instance, you can now require a mobile phone number or require one or many custom fields when a FusionAuth admin is creating a user.
 You can also modify the registration creation and update form in the FusionAuth admin UI, including capturing custom data, removing unused fields and adding validation to the custom fields. This form is configurable on an application by application basis.
 For instance, if your business logic needs to know a user’s favorite color (or anything else), FusionAuth has you covered:
  When combined with FusionAuth’s granular roles, which allow you to create users with limited privileges, you can expose these forms to customer service reps or other users whom you might not want to access the entire FusionAuth admin UI.
  In addition to these features, there were over ten other bugs squashed and GitHub issues resolved as well. These fixes include changes to JWT contents in certain situations, dashboard user counts, and superfluous log messages. Please see the  release notes for the full breakdown of the changes between 1.19 and 1.20.
 If you’d like to upgrade your self-hosted FusionAuth instance, see our  upgrade guide. If you have a FusionAuth Cloud deployment, open a  support request from your account dashboard or  use our contact form and we’ll get your servers upgraded! Or, if you’d like to download and use FusionAuth,  check out your options.
title: FusionAuth 1.20

这一想法是一项关于跑步和步行对膝关节不同影响的有趣新研究的核心。通过动作捕捉和复杂的计算机建模，这项研究证实了跑步比步行对膝盖的影响更大。但作者总结说，在这个过程中，跑步可能也会加强和增大软骨，软骨是一种缓冲骨骼末端的橡胶组织。这些发现提出了一种令人着迷的可能性，即跑步可能会增强膝盖的力量，帮助延缓膝关节炎，而不是伤害膝盖。
当然，跑步会伤及膝盖的观念是广泛而根深蒂固的。几乎每个跑步的人都熟悉好心的、不跑步的家人、朋友和陌生人的警告，他们的膝盖注定要完蛋了。
这种担忧并不是没有根据的。跑步涉及到大量的关节弯曲和撞击，这会磨损膝盖内的缓冲软骨。软骨没有自己的血液供应，通常被认为在受损时几乎没有自我修复的能力，或者在童年后根本没有太大的变化。因此，可以想象，反复跑步会磨损脆弱的软骨，几乎不可避免地会导致严重的膝关节炎。
但在现实生活中，情况并非如此。有些跑步者会患膝关节炎，但不是全部。事实上，从统计上看，作为一个群体，跑步者比不跑步者患关节炎的可能性更小。
为什么跑步节省了这么多跑步者的膝盖，这个问题长期以来一直让马里兰大学位于学院公园的运动学副教授罗斯·米勒(Ross Miller)感兴趣。在早期的研究中，他和他的同事们通过让志愿者沿着装有板子的轨道行走和跑步，以测量每一步产生的力，从而研究了跑步机械是否重要。
由此得出的数据显示，人们在跑步时更猛烈地撞击地面，每迈一步膝盖受到的撞击要大得多。但他们在两步之间花在高空的时间也更多，这意味着他们在行走相同距离的情况下走得更少。因此，研究人员得出结论，随着时间的推移，通过他们膝盖的累积力应该是大致相同的，无论是步行还是跑步。
但是，最近，米勒博士开始怀疑这一发现是否真的解释了为什么跑步不会伤及更多的膝盖。他知道，最近对动物的一些研究暗示，软骨可能比研究人员之前认为的更有弹性。在这些研究中，跑步的动物往往比久坐不动的动物的可比组织有更厚、更健康的膝关节软骨，这表明活跃的动物的软骨随着跑步的反应而发生了变化。
米勒博士推测，也许人类跑步者膝盖中的软骨同样会发生改变和适应。
为了找出答案，他再次要求一群健康的年轻男女沿着一条包含力板的轨道行走和跑步，同时他和他的同事们对他们进行了拍摄。然后，研究人员计算了志愿者在散步和跑步时产生的力。最后，他们对志愿者膝盖的未来进行了建模。
更具体地说，他们使用了力板数字，加上过去在实验室中拉动和敲打直到软骨分崩离析的活组织研究的大量额外数据，以及其他来源来创建计算机模拟。他们想看看，理论上，如果一个成年人多年来每天步行6公里(约3.7英里)，与之相比，如果他们每天步行3公里，再跑3公里，健康的膝关节软骨会发生什么。
他们还测试了另外两种理论情况。首先，研究人员设定了这样一种可能性，即人们的膝盖软骨在行走或跑步造成的反复小损伤后会轻微修复--但不会发生其他变化。对于最后一种情况，他们假设软骨会主动地自我重塑，适应移动的要求，变得更厚、更强壮，就像我们锻炼肌肉时所做的那样。
模特们的最终结果令人大开眼界。根据模拟，如果模型不包括膝盖软骨自我适应或修复的可能性，每天的步行者在55岁前患关节炎的可能性约为36%。如果假设软骨能够修复或适应，这一风险下降到约13%，这大约是研究预测的其他健康人在现实世界中关节炎的风险。
竞选的数字更令人担忧。当模型假设软骨不会改变时，跑步者最终患关节炎的风险高达98%，如果模型考虑到软骨修复的可能性，风险只会下降到95%。实际上，根据这种情况，频繁跑步对软骨的损害将压倒组织自我修复的任何能力。
但是，如果模型包括人们跑步时软骨主动适应的可能性-变得更厚、更柔软-跑步者患关节炎的几率下降到约13%，与健康的步行者相同。
罗斯博士说，这些结果表明软骨是有延展性的。它必须能够感觉到跑步带来的紧张和轻微的损伤，并重建自己，变得更强壮。在这种情况下，跑步有助于软骨健康。
然而，像这样的模型化结果只是理论上的，而且是有限的。他们没有解释在没有血液供应的情况下软骨是如何自我重塑的，也没有解释遗传、营养、体重、膝盖损伤和其他因素是否会影响个人的关节炎风险。这样的模型也没有告诉我们不同的距离、速度或跑步形式是否会改变结果。米勒博士说，为了了解更多，我们需要直接测量跑步后活体人类软骨的分子和其他变化，但这样的测试很困难。
尽管如此，这项研究可能会平息一些跑步者的疑虑--以及他们的家人和朋友的疑虑。“看起来跑步不太可能因为软骨磨损而导致膝关节炎，”罗斯博士说。
note_en:
That idea is at the heart of a  fascinating new study of the differing effects of running and walking on the knee joint. Using motion capture and sophisticated computer modeling, the study confirms that running pummels knees more than walking does. But in the process, the authors conclude, running likely also fortifies and bulks up the cartilage, the rubbery tissue that cushions the ends of bones. The findings raise the beguiling possibility that, instead of harming knees, running might fortify them and help to stave off knee arthritis.
 Of course, the notion that running wrecks knees is widespread and entrenched. Almost anyone who runs is familiar with warnings from well-meaning, nonrunning family members, friends and strangers that their knees are doomed.
 This concern is not unwarranted. Running involves substantial joint bending and pounding, which can fray the cushioning cartilage inside the knee. Cartilage, which does not have its own blood supply, generally is thought to have little ability to repair itself when damaged or to change much at all after childhood. So, repeated running conceivably wears away fragile cartilage and almost inevitably should lead to crippling knee arthritis.
 But in real life, it does not. Some runners develop knee arthritis, but not all. As a group, in fact,  runners may be statistically less likely to become arthritic than nonrunners.
 The question of why running spares so many runners’ knees has long intrigued Ross Miller, an associate professor of kinesiology at the University of Maryland in College Park. In earlier research, he and his colleagues had looked into whether running mechanics matter, by asking volunteers to walk and run along a track outfitted with plates to measure the forces generated with each step.
 The resulting data showed that people hit the ground harder while running, clobbering their knees far more with each stride. But they also spent more time aloft between strides, meaning they took fewer strides while covering the same distance as when walking. So, the cumulative forces moving through their knees over time  should be about the same, the researchers concluded, whether someone walked or ran.
 But, recently, Dr. Miller had begun to doubt whether this finding really explained why running wasn’t wrecking more knees. He knew that some recent studies with animals intimated that cartilage might be more resilient than researchers previously had believed. In those studies, animals that ran tended to have thicker, healthier knee cartilage than comparable tissues from sedentary animals, suggesting that the active animals’ cartilage had changed in response to their running.
  Perhaps, Dr. Miller speculated, cartilage in human runners’ knees likewise might alter and adapt.
 To find out, he again asked a group of healthy young men and women to walk and run along a track containing force plates, while he and his colleagues filmed them. The researchers then computed the forces the volunteers had generated while strolling and running. Finally, they modeled what the future might hold for the volunteers’ knees.
 More specifically, they used the force-plate numbers, plus extensive additional data from past studies of biopsied cartilage pulled and pummeled in the lab until it fell apart and other sources to create computer simulations. They wanted to see what, theoretically, would happen to healthy knee cartilage if an adult walked for six kilometers (about 3.7 miles) every day for years, compared to if they walked for three kilometers and ran for another three kilometers each of those days.
 They also tested two additional theoretical situations. For one, the researchers programmed in the possibility that people’s knee cartilage would slightly repair itself after repeated small damage from walking or running — but not otherwise change. And for the last scenario, they presumed that the cartilage would actively remodel itself and adapt to the demands of moving, growing thicker and stronger, much as muscle does when we exercise.
 The models’ final results were eye-opening. According to the simulations, daily walkers faced about a 36 percent chance of developing arthritis by the age of 55, if the model did not include the possibility of the knee cartilage adapting or repairing itself. That risk dropped to about 13 percent if cartilage were assumed to be able to repair or adapt, which is about what studies predict to be the real-world arthritis risk for otherwise healthy people.
 The numbers for running were more worrisome. When the model assumed cartilage cannot change, the runners’ risk of eventual arthritis was a whopping 98 percent, declining only to 95 percent if the model factored in the possibility of cartilage repair. In effect, according to this scenario, the damage to cartilage from frequent running would overwhelm any ability of the tissue to fix itself.
 But if the model included the likelihood of the cartilage actively adapting — growing thicker and cushier — when people ran, the odds of runners developing arthritis fell to about 13 percent, the same as for healthy walkers.
 What these results suggest is that cartilage is malleable, Dr. Ross says. It must be able to sense the strains and slight damage from running and rebuild itself, becoming stronger. In this scenario, running bolsters cartilage health.
 Modeled results like these are theoretical, though, and limited. They do not explain how cartilage remodels itself without a blood supply or if genetics, nutrition, body weight, knee injuries and other factors affect individual arthritis risks. Such models also do not tell us if different distances, speeds or running forms would alter the outcomes. To learn more, we will need direct measures of molecular and other changes in living human cartilage after running, Dr. Miller says, but such tests are difficult.
 Still, this study may quiet some runners’ qualms — and those of their families and friends. “It looks like running is unlikely to cause knee arthritis by wearing out cartilage,” Dr. Ross says.
title: 为什么跑步不会毁了你的膝盖
title_en: Why running won't ruin your knees

note:
名为Serial 1 Cycle Company的新业务最初是摩托车制造商产品开发中心内的一个项目。这个名字来源于“序列号1”，这是哈雷戴维森已知最古老的摩托车的昵称。
踏板辅助电动自行车公司是在新冠肺炎疫情后需求不断增长的推动下蓬勃发展的电动自行车行业中成立的。根据哈雷-戴维森(Harley-Davidson)的数据，2019年全球电动自行车市场估计将超过150亿美元，预计从2020年到2025年的年增长率将超过6%。
新的哈雷戴维森品牌Serial 1没有提供其新的电动自行车系列产品的性能细节或其他规格。然而，该公司确实发布了几张第一款车型的照片。
这项新业务的推出也正值这家总部位于密尔沃基的摩托车制造商的关键时刻，由于其核心客户基础老化，其摩托车销售放缓。
今年7月，哈雷-戴维森(Harley-Davidson)在全球业务部门裁员700人，这是一项名为“改头换面”的内部品牌重组计划的一部分。哈雷-戴维森董事长、总裁兼首席执行官约亨·泽茨(Jochen Zeitz)在4月份公司第一季度收益电话会议上首次谈到了这一计划，此前该公司推出了第一款量产电动摩托车Livewire。
新公司品牌总监亚伦·弗兰克(Aaron Frank)在一份声明中表示：“Serial 1的成立使哈雷-戴维森在这场移动性革命中发挥了关键作用，同时允许Serial 1专注于电动自行车客户，并提供植根于自由和冒险的无与伦比的骑行体验，”新公司的品牌总监亚伦·弗兰克(Aaron Frank)在一份声明中表示。
哈雷-戴维森说，杰森·洪博培是Serial 1 Cycle的总裁。高管团队的其余成员包括产品开发副总裁本·隆德(Ben Lund)和首席品牌营销专家汉娜·阿尔滕伯格(Hannah Altenburg)。
note_en:
The new business called Serial 1 Cycle Company started as a project within the motorcycle manufacturer’s product development center. The name comes from “Serial Number One,” the nickname for Harley-Davidson’s oldest known motorcycle.
 The pedal assist electric bicycle company is being launched amid a booming ebike industry fueled by growing demand in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic. The global eBicycle market was estimated to be over $15 billion in 2019 and projected to grow at an annual rate of more than 6% from 2020 to 2025, according to Harley-Davidson.
  The new Harley-Davidson brand Serial 1 didn’t provide performance details or other specs of its new line of electric bike products. However, the company did release several photos of its first model.
  The new business launch also comes at a critical time for the Milwaukee-based motorcycle manufacturer, which has seen its sales slow as its core customer base ages out of its motorcycles.
 In July, Harley-Davidson cut 700 jobs from its global operations as part of an internally branded restructuring plan called “The Rewire.” The plan, which Harley-Davidson chairman, president and CEO Jochen Zeitz first spoke about in the company’s first-quarter earnings call back in April, followed the launch of the company’s first production electric motorcycle the Livewire.
 “The formation of Serial 1 allows Harley-Davidson to play a key role in this mobility revolution while allowing Serial 1 to focus exclusively on the eBicycle customer and deliver an unmatched riding experience rooted in freedom and adventure,” Aaron Frank, the new company’s brand director said in a statement.
 Harley-Davidson said Jason Huntsman is president of Serial 1 Cycle. The rest of the executive team includes Ben Lund, who is vice president of product development and Hannah Altenburg as lead brand marketing specialist.
title: 哈雷戴维森正在进军电动自行车行业。
title_en: Harley-Davidson is getting into the electric bicycle business

note:
与最近其他备受瞩目的科技听证会相比，参议院听证会的范围将更窄，更以政策为中心，具体关注通信体面法案(Communications Decency Act)第230条。这项简短的法律可能听起来晦涩难懂，但它是保护互联网公司免受其托管的用户生成内容(从Facebook帖子和推文到Yelp评论和评论部分)责任的关键法律盾牌。
最近的大型科技听证会一直在曲折进行，很少会迫使一些世界上最强大的公司的领导人透露太多信息。但是，联邦反垄断行动的累积压力，不到一周后的高风险选举，以及一些可能废除让他们的业务成为可能的法律的立法提案，可能会设定不同的基调-并有望提供更多实质内容。
你可以在这里(上图)关注听证会的实况转播，从美国东部时间10月28日(星期三)上午10点开始。我们将关注证词和第230条的所有内容，所以请回来查看我们今天的主要外卖报道。
note_en:
The Senate hearing will have a narrower, more policy-centric scope than other recent high profile tech hearings, focusing specifically on  Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act. That short law might sound obscure, but it’s the  key legal shield that protects internet companies from liability for the user-generated content they host, from Facebook posts and tweets to Yelp reviews and comments sections.
   Recent big tech hearings have meandered, seldom forcing the leaders of some of the world’s most powerful companies into revealing much. But the cumulative pressure of  federal antitrust action, a  high-stakes election less than a week away and a  number of legislative proposals that could dismantle the law that made their businesses possible will likely set a different tone — and hopefully offer more substance.
 You can follow a livestream of the hearing here (above) starting at 10:00 AM ET on Wednesday, October 28. We’ll be following the testimony and all things Section 230, so check back for our coverage of the day’s key takeaways.
title: 观看Facebook、Google和Twitter的首席执行官们为这项使国会能够使用社交媒体的法律辩护
title_en: Watch Facebook, Google, and Twitter’s CEOs defend the law that made social media possible to Congress
